---
title: "Stata + R Integration: Regional Analysis"
author: ""
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    embed-resources: true
---

<!--
================================================================================
FULL SCRIPT ROADMAP
================================================================================

PART 1 — STATA
  1.1  Load raw microdata (use "path/to/data.dta", clear)
  1.2  Summary statistics
         - summarize key variables
         - tabulate categorical variables
  1.3  Box plot of outcome variable by region (graph box ...)
  1.4  Lorenz curve + Gini coefficient (ssc install lorenz, then: lorenz ...)
  1.5  Aggregate to subregional level
         - collapse (mean) (sum) ..., by(region subregion)
  1.6  Export subregional CSV
         - export delimited "output/subregional_data.csv", replace

PART 2 — R
  2.1  Install / load packages: tidyverse, sf, tmap / ggplot2 + ggspatial
  2.2  Load subregional CSV produced by Stata
  2.3  Load shapefile (st_read)
  2.4  Join CSV data to shapefile on region/subregion key
  2.5  Map 1: choropleth of statistic A (e.g. mean income)
  2.6  Map 2: choropleth of statistic B (e.g. Gini / share)
  2.7  Arrange maps side by side (patchwork or tmap_arrange)
  2.8  Save output (tmap_save / ggsave)

================================================================================
-->

# Part 1: Stata — Load Data, EDA & Export

## 1.1 Load Data

```{stata}
* TODO: replace with actual path
* use "path/to/your/data.dta", clear

* --- PLACEHOLDER SNIPPET: synthetic data for testing ---
clear
set obs 200
set seed 42

gen region     = ceil(_n / 50)                          // 4 regions
gen subregion  = ceil(_n / 10)                          // 20 subregions
gen income     = exp(rnormal(8, 0.6))                   // log-normal income
gen weight     = runiform(0.5, 2)                       // sampling weights

label var region    "Region (1-4)"
label var subregion "Subregion (1-20)"
label var income    "Household income"
label var weight    "Sampling weight"

list in 1/5
```
